response: the complex number z satisfies iz = sqrt(3) - i, we can solve for z by dividing both sides by i. this gives z = (sqrt(3) - i)/i. to simplify, multiply numerator and denominator by -i to rationalize the denominator: z = (sqrt(3) - i)(-i)/(-i^2). since i^2 = -1, the denominator becomes 1. expanding the numerator, we get z = (-i*sqrt(3) + i^2)/1. substituting i^2 = -1, z = (-i*sqrt(3) - 1). the magnitude of z is the square root of the sum of the squares of the real and imaginary parts. the real part is -1 and the imaginary part is -sqrt(3)i. so |z| = sqrt((-1)^2 + (-sqrt(3))^2) = sqrt(1 + 3) = sqrt(4) = 2. is there anything else i can help with?
